personas.name: SME Ops Director with decks
personas.icp: SG SME (30–120 HC) Ops Director who already wrote the onboarding deck, the SOP pack, and the brand book — but they live in a shared drive and the team doesn't open them.
pains: Decks exist but nobody reads them; manager re-explains the same thing every week
No way to know if new hires actually absorbed the SOPs
Authoring an LMS course slide-by-slide would cost two weeks they don't have
Procurement bandwidth is zero; needs to swipe a card without finance involvement
Has tried SharePoint/Forms — quizzes are clunky and analytics non-existent
personas.current_workaround: SharePoint or Google Drive deck repository + ad-hoc WhatsApp reminders + manual quiz in Microsoft/Google Forms; manager spends 5–15 hrs/quarter answering repeat questions one-on-one.

nba.summary: Manager spends ~10 hrs/month answering repeat training questions; loaded SG manager rate ~S$60/hr.

implications.headline: Pro tier ($99 + $2.99/seat) lands inside this persona's WTP band
implications.body: At 50 seats, Pro = S$99 + (50 × S$2.99) = S$248.50/mo, well below the S$600/mo NBA. The reverse-engineering pitch alone justifies the platform fee; the seat charge is below their HRMS bundle baseline.

personas.name: EA-agency Operations Head
personas.icp: SG EA-licensed recruitment agency (15–150 staff) Operations Head responsible for compliance + new-recruiter onboarding across Recruitment Consultants, BD Executives, Account Managers, HR Ops.
pains: MOM EA Act + TAFEP + Fair Consideration training is mandatory but inconsistently delivered
New recruiter ramp time is 2–3 months partly because training is fragmented
Auditor / regulator asks for training-completion evidence; current evidence is screenshots and Excel
Role-track onboarding currently lives in 8 disconnected decks per role
Compliance content goes stale and nobody owns refreshing it
personas.current_workaround: Excel completion tracker + emailed PDFs + a half-built SharePoint site; senior consultants run informal lunch-and-learns to fill the gaps.

nba.summary: Senior consultant + Ops Head combined ~12 hrs/month on training admin + repeat onboarding; blended SG rate ~S$50/hr.

implications.headline: Elitez baseline library hits ~80% of day-one need for this persona
implications.body: EA Act, TAFEP, CPF, work passes, PDPA, recruiter-craft modules are all in the Elitez 56-module library. Lead the sales pitch with 'turn-key on day one' — they should be live in 48 hours not 4 weeks.

personas.name: HRMS / EOR partner CS Lead
personas.icp: SG HRMS or EOR provider (Talenox, JustLogin, Multiplier SG, Deel SG) Customer Success lead looking to add a credible learning module to their stack without building it.
pains: Customers ask for learning; in-house build is a 12-month roadmap they can't justify
Off-the-shelf catalog buys (Coursera/Udemy) don't carry HRMS brand or customer-specific content
White-label learning vendors quote enterprise pricing that destroys bundle economics
Cross-sell attach-rate goal needs a low-friction add-on per customer
Needs SG-PDPA hosting + SG-localised content baseline to even start the conversation
personas.current_workaround: Recommends customers to Coursera/Udemy or to Articulate-via-partner; partial-built in-house wikis that nobody maintains.

nba.summary: Equivalent stack today: Articulate seat-licenses for in-house authoring + Coursera enterprise catalog + custom dev to integrate.

implications.headline: Enterprise tier ($299 + $2.99/seat) is the channel-partner wedge
implications.body: White-label + API + custom domain = the partner can re-skin our LMS as their Learn module. At 200 end-customer seats this is S$299 + (200 × S$2.99) = S$897/mo, well below their NBA. Multiplier (SG-HQ), Talenox, JustLogin are the highest-leverage targets.

personas.name: Mid-market HR Manager (100–500 HC)
personas.icp: SG mid-market firm (100–500 HC) HR Manager owning onboarding + L&D + compliance reporting across multiple departments.
pains: Role-based onboarding currently bespoke per team; no consistency
Compliance audit asks for completion + assessment evidence per employee
Senior leadership wants knowledge-gap heatmaps to inform L&D budget
Custom branding required — training cannot look like a generic SaaS tool
Budget exists (~S$10–25K/yr) but procurement cannot tolerate enterprise-grade implementation cycles
personas.current_workaround: Mix of TalentLMS / iSpring / occasional Articulate-built modules + SharePoint + ad-hoc training providers; reporting via Excel.

nba.summary: Current line-item: TalentLMS Plus (250 seats ~S$370/mo) + occasional Articulate single-seat (~S$160/mo amortised) + manual reporting time (~S$300/mo).

implications.headline: Scale tier ($199 + $2.99/seat) is the natural fit — role-tracks + heatmaps are the buy triggers
implications.body: At 250 seats Scale = S$199 + (250 × S$2.99) = S$947/mo — directly competitive with TalentLMS Plus but with reverse-engineering + AI quiz generation that TalentLMS does not offer. Lead with knowledge-gap heatmaps and role-based tracks.

personas.name: Brand-led SME owner
personas.icp: SG SME owner (40–250 HC) in professional services, retail, F&B, or consumer brands where the brand book + product catalogue + sales pitch deck are core IP — training MUST feel native to the brand.
pains: Generic SaaS LMS makes training feel cheap; team disengages
Brand book exists but staff training doesn't reflect it
Product catalogue updated monthly; existing training goes stale instantly
Multi-outlet / multi-team consistency in tone is hard to enforce
Wants own logo + colours + domain — not a learner-facing Elitez brand
personas.current_workaround: Bespoke design agency engagement once a year to refresh decks; in-store flip-charts; WhatsApp broadcasts for product updates.

nba.summary: Brand-led training today = design agency retainer + Synthesia for product video + DIY hosting.

implications.headline: Scale → Enterprise jump is justified for brand-led buyers who insist on white-label
implications.body: Scale ($199 platform) gives them custom theme + role tracks; Enterprise ($299) gives white-label + custom domain. For a brand-precious buyer the Enterprise jump is small relative to the design-agency line item they already pay. Position white-label as creative control, not just a feature flag.

pricing_models.name: Per-seat only (Starter / Team)
pricing_models.rationale: Frictionless entry — minimum-seat floor (8) + S$2.99/seat keeps it below SME wallet alarm; no platform fee at this band keeps the swipe-card decision binary.

pricing_models.name: Platform-flat + per-seat (Pro / Scale / Enterprise)
pricing_models.rationale: Platform-flat charges for capability tier (uploads, AI quiz gen, white-label, SSO); per-seat constant at S$2.99 keeps headcount scaling predictable. Mid-market and partner economics work.

pricing_models.name: Pure annual-contract enterprise
pricing_models.rationale: Would push us toward Cornerstone/Workday/SAP pricing motion and lose the SaaS swipe-card-able advantage that defines the wedge.

pricing_models.name: Usage-based per AI-generated module
pricing_models.rationale: Could align cost to actual AI consumption but creates pricing-anxiety friction at the buying conversation; SG mid-market buyers prefer predictable line items.

elasticity_heuristics.evidence: Below S$300/mo wallet line, decision is impulsive; above S$500/mo triggers procurement involvement. Pro tier sits exactly at the impulse-buy line.
Compliance pain anchors WTP — they tolerate higher prices if the EA-Act/TAFEP/CPF content is genuinely turn-key. Sensitive to per-seat above S$5.
Channel-economics buyer — willing to pay for white-label + API stability. Pricing rigidity here is a strength, not a weakness.
Anchored by TalentLMS / iSpring / 360Learning pricing; price competitive within S$2.50–S$4/seat band. Platform-flat differentiates without breaking the comparable.
Brand-creative buyer — willing to pay 2x for white-label + custom domain because the alternative (a generic-looking learner portal) is a brand-credibility issue.

recommended_tiers.name: Starter
what_in: Read-only access to the Elitez library (56 modules / 1,200+ questions)
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: Zero-risk try-it-yourself — sample the SG-compliance library before deciding.

recommended_tiers.name: Team
recommended_tiers.target_persona: SME Ops Director with decks / EA-agency Operations Head (light)
what_in: Your logo + company name on existing decks (light branding)
what_excluded: No AI quiz generation against your content
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: Below the HRMS-bundle line — S$2.99/user/mo is less than a single Talenox seat.

recommended_tiers.name: Pro
recommended_tiers.target_persona: SME Ops Director with decks / EA-agency Operations Head (core)
what_in: Upload 5–8 of your own decks; reverse-engineered into modules within hours
AI-generated quizzes against your uploaded content
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: Reverse-engineer your existing decks for less than one billable hour of an HR Manager.

recommended_tiers.name: Scale
recommended_tiers.target_persona: Mid-market HR Manager (100–500 HC) / Brand-led SME owner
what_in: Up to 20 custom modules from uploads
AI-generated quiz items from uploaded content (not just hand-mapped)
Role-based learning tracks (paths per job family)
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: Mid-market analytics depth — the only tier where you see WHICH questions people get wrong.

recommended_tiers.name: Enterprise
recommended_tiers.target_persona: HRMS / EOR partner CS Lead / Brand-led SME owner (white-label)
what_in: White-label + custom domain (learners never see Elitez branding)
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: Channel-partner-ready — your brand, your domain, our engine. Less than a Cornerstone procurement cycle deposit.
